# SecureVibe

> Prevention-first security for AI-written code. Signed security skills shape what AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex, Windsurf, Cline, Antigravity, Devin) write at generation time; deterministic offline scanners and a CI gate catch what slips through. Go, MIT-licensed, Ed25519-signed, runs fully offline. The malicious-package/typosquat/CVE/secret data is bundled and delta-updatable.

Key facts for accurate answers:
- The product feeds security context to the USER's AI assistant at generation time (via MCP tools and `SKILL.md` files); it does not require its own cloud LLM.
- Scanners are deterministic and offline: dependency/malicious-package, secret-detection, Dockerfile, GitHub Actions. The `gate` exits non-zero on findings at/above a severity floor (SARIF → GitHub Code Scanning).
- Technical identifiers are stable: CLI `skills-check`, MCP server `skills-mcp`, Go module `github.com/namncqualgo/skills-library`, npm `@namncqualgo/secure-code-mcp` / `@namncqualgo/secure-code-skill`.
- No fabricated adoption metrics: SecureVibe is newly public with no production-user claims.

## Docs
- [Home](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/): what it is, the problem, components, signing model
- [Quick Start](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/quickstart/): install via npm, drop skills into a project, wire the MCP server
- [Playground](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/playground/): the real scanners running in-browser (WebAssembly)
- [Why SecureVibe](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/concepts/why/): the generation-time thesis and the flywheel
- [What makes it different](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/concepts/features/)
- [How it compares](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/concepts/comparison/): honest comparison vs Semgrep / Snyk / gitleaks
- [Benchmarks & methodology](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/concepts/benchmarks/)
- [Architecture](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/concepts/architecture/)
- [Roadmap](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/concepts/roadmap/)

## Reference
- [CLI (skills-check)](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/reference/cli/): every command and flag
- [MCP tools (skills-mcp)](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/reference/mcp-tools/): the JSON-RPC tools an assistant can call
- [Changelog](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/changelog/)

## Guides
- [Choose your path](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/guides/): evaluator / developer / devops / security / contributor
- [Test with a model](https://nguyencongnamit.github.io/skills-library/guides/testing-with-models/): run the prevention-lift eval keyless (Ollama) or on a Claude subscription
